mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a self-hosted AI platform designed for user-friendly interactions with various LLM runners. It allows seamless integration with OpenAI-compatible APIs and provides a powerful offline deployment solution. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and management compared to cloud-based solutions.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI to manage the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

```

> This command downloads the CLI and makes it executable.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> Install Open WebUI in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for Open WebUI.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command sets up autoscaling for the deployment.
> This command sets up monitoring for the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
This will remove Open WebUI and all associated resources. Be aware that persistent data may be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> Uninstall the Open WebUI Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl cp open-webui-pod:/path/to/config /local/backup/config

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> Backup existing configuration files.
> Upgrade Open WebUI to the latest version.
> Verify that the Open WebUI pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 0.0.0.0:443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to a misconfigured kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure the correct context is set with 'kubectl config use-context CONTEXT_NAME'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "open-webui/open-webui:v0.8.8": Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image does not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your Helm chart values.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on Open WebUI pod
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs POD_NAME' and ensure all required configurations are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the Open WebUI deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved API integration capabilities, and enhanced user permissions management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Open WebUI dashboard accessible at http://<your-cluster-ip>:<port> with a successful login prompt.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui
gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up with the necessary tools installed.
